last_verdict.tests: 1958 passed, 25 skipped (.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q). FIVE waves + a LIVE test this session. Wave 5: LIVE-validated asr-tts-2 (async second pass ENGAGED, 0 underflows/exceptions/crashes/self-interrupts, barge-in cuts real talk-overs) BUT surfaced two problems: (1) the MIC IS CLIPPING 36-65% of blocks (peak=1.0, rms~0.5) -> OS mic gain too hot -> garbled STT + SenseVoice hallucinations ('I.'); OWNER must lower OS mic input level/disable mic-boost (input_gain=1.0 so it's not code). (2) owner CONFIRMED real acoustic OUTPUT artifacts (buzzy/glitchy TTS, not just garbled words) = the run-103630 white-noise still partly present; _audio_cb silently zero-fills FIFO underruns. ADDED DIAGNOSTICS (real-time-safe, reviewed SHIP): input-clipping warning in the capture heartbeat (clip=% + one-shot WARN >2%), output-underrun counter in _audio_cb (per-reply WARN + playback_underrun metric + heartbeat underruns=N). NEXT: owner lowers mic gain + re-runs -> clip% should ~0 (STT jumps) and underruns=N confirms whether the buzzy artifacts are FIFO underruns from CPU contention (async SenseVoice worker + TTS synth on the desktop CPU tier) -> if so, per-tier TTS/worker thread budgeting. Also: auto-detect picked 'desktop' not 'desktop_gpu_4090' (probe reads 16376MB < 16GB threshold -- recommend_profile off-by-rounding). --- Earlier waves 1-4: Wave 4 (low-value headless cleanup): llm-inference-9 (LlamaCppLLM type_k/type_v KV-cache quant plumbing; phone/phone_lite quantize the K cache to q8_0, V left at f16 since V-quant needs flash-attn; fail-soft on an old lib) + control-plane-2 (load-elastic admission: supervisor caps concurrency to 1 under sustained load via an ungated load_fraction reader threaded app->runtime->supervisor, separate from the live_routing-gated load_snapshot). DEFERRED with reasons: llm-inference-6 (per-call timeout blocked by the single-context lock; supervisor 25s reap backstops it), llm-inference-7 (Q3 weights config flip needs on-device quality validation). Reviewed (1 refuted, nits+1 LOW; LOW fixed -> K-only). --- Wave 3: Wave 3 (headless): control-plane-3 -- EWMA-scaled ADAPTIVE watchdog deadlines. The recorder now folds a TTS first-audio EWMA (recent_tts_ms) alongside the TTFT one; the watchdog's 'llm stuck'/'tts stuck' deadlines = clamp(4*recent_ewma, floor, ceil) with cold-start fallback to the static 10s/5s. LLM floor 6s (above a heavy-tier first-token, since the EWMA blends tiers), ceil 30s; ADAPTIVE_DEADLINES toggle pins legacy. Net: fast device surfaces a real stall sooner, slow phone stops false-flagging honest long turns (attacks the stuck:1 bundle noise). Diagnosis only -- supervisor task-reap is the real hang bound. Adversarially reviewed (0 refuted, nits+1 LOW, fixed). Scouted first: routing-cascade-6 already neutralized by LLMCapabilityRouter cache, routing-cascade-7 done, routing-cascade-4/asr-tts-3 need real models/audio. --- Wave 1+2: Wave 1: asr-tts-2 (async second-pass ASR) + asr-tts-1 (per-tier ASR policy). Wave 2 (headless, no live test): llm-inference-3 (LlamaCppLLM num_predict->max_tokens cap translation + phone/phone_lite caps) + rc-5 (SUPERSEDED metric so newest-input-wins-preempted turns don't false-flag 'llm stuck') + invariant hardening (no profile may disable speaker_gate_input / agent_brain.require_owner_verified). Both waves adversarially reviewed; wave-2 review CAUGHT A BLOCKER (a config _comment key leaking into llama.cpp's strict create_chat_completion signature) -> fixed by dropping _-prefixed option keys + a strict-signature regression test. Scouted Phase-1 first: device-adapt-1/cross-platform-8/cloud-invariant were already done.
